SEARCH
Package io.github.lucfr1746.llibrary.itemstack

Class ItemBuilder

java.lang.Object
io.github.lucfr1746.llibrary.itemstack.ItemBuilder
public class ItemBuilder extends Object
A utility class for building and modifying ItemStack objects. This class provides methods to easily create and customize ItemStack instances.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ItemBuilder(@NotNull org.bukkit.Material material)
    Constructs an ItemBuilder with a Material and a default amount of 1.
    ItemBuilder(@NotNull org.bukkit.Material material, int amount)
    Constructs an ItemBuilder with a Material and a specified amount.
    ItemBuilder(@NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack itemStack)
    Constructs an ItemBuilder with a predefined ItemStack.

  • Constructor Details

    • ItemBuilder

      public ItemBuilder(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack itemStack)
      Constructs an ItemBuilder with a predefined ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      itemStack - The ItemStack to wrap. Cannot be null.

    • ItemBuilder

      public ItemBuilder(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.Material material, int amount)
      Constructs an ItemBuilder with a Material and a specified amount.
      Parameters:
      material - The Material of the item. Cannot be null.
      amount - The amount of the item. Must be between 1 and 127 (inclusive).
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the amount is invalid.

    • ItemBuilder

      public ItemBuilder(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.Material material)
      Constructs an ItemBuilder with a Material and a default amount of 1.
      Parameters:
      material - The Material of the item. Cannot be null.

  • Method Details

    • build

      @NotNull public @NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack build()
      Returns the ItemStack that this builder wraps.
      Returns:
      The ItemStack instance. Never null.

    • setAmount

      public ItemBuilder setAmount(int amount)
      Sets the amount of the ItemStack. If the amount is less than 1 or greater than 127, an exception is thrown. If the amount is negative, the current amount is adjusted, but not below 0.
      Parameters:
      amount - The amount to set or adjust by.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the amount exceeds bounds (1-127) or if the item is invalid.

    • getAmount

      public int getAmount()
      Gets the current amount of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      The amount of the item, or -1 if the item is invalid.

    • getItemAttributeBuilder

      public ItemAttributeBuilder getItemAttributeBuilder()
      Creates and returns an ItemAttributeBuilder for further customization of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      An ItemAttributeBuilder instance for the item, or null if the item is invalid.

    • getAxolotlVariant

      public org.bukkit.entity.Axolotl.Variant getAxolotlVariant()
      Retrieves the Axolotl.Variant from the ItemStack if it contains AxolotlBucketMeta.
      Returns:
      The Axolotl.Variant associated with the item, or null if the item is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack's meta is not an instance of AxolotlBucketMeta.

    • hasAxolotlVariant

      public boolean hasAxolotlVariant()
      Checks if the ItemStack with AxolotlBucketMeta contains an Axolotl.Variant.
      Returns:
      true if the ItemStack has an Axolotl.Variant, false otherwise, false if the item is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack's meta is not an instance of AxolotlBucketMeta.

    • setAxolotlVariant

      public ItemBuilder setAxolotlVariant(@NotNull org.bukkit.entity.Axolotl.Variant axolotlVariant)
      Sets the Axolotl.Variant for the ItemStack if it contains AxolotlBucketMeta.
      Parameters:
      axolotlVariant - The Axolotl.Variant to set. Cannot be null.
      Returns:
      The current ItemBuilder instance for method chaining, or null if the item is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack's meta is not an instance of AxolotlBucketMeta.

    • getBannerBuilder

      public BannerBuilder getBannerBuilder()
      Converts the current ItemStack into a BannerBuilder. The ItemStack is passed into the BannerBuilder constructor to allow for pattern modifications on the banner.
      Returns:
      a new BannerBuilder instance, initialized with the current ItemStack.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the ItemStack is invalid or does not have BannerMeta.

    • getBookBuilder

      public BookBuilder getBookBuilder()
      Converts the current ItemStack into a BookBuilder. The ItemStack is passed into the BookBuilder constructor to allow for further modifications on the book.
      Returns:
      a new BookBuilder instance, initialized with the current ItemStack.

    • getColor

      public org.bukkit.Color getColor()
      Retrieves the color of the ItemStack if applicable.
      Returns:
      the color of the ItemStack, or null if not applicable.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not support color.

    • hasColor

      public boolean hasColor()
      Checks if the ItemStack has a color.
      Returns:
      true if the ItemStack has a color, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not support color.

    • setColor

      public ItemBuilder setColor(org.bukkit.Color color)
      Sets the color of the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      color - the color to set.
      Returns:
      the updated ItemBuilder instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not support color.

    • setColor

      public ItemBuilder setColor(int red, int green, int blue)
      Sets the color of the ItemStack using RGB values.
      Parameters:
      red - the red component (0-255).
      green - the green component (0-255).
      blue - the blue component (0-255).
      Returns:
      the updated ItemBuilder instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any, RGB value is out of range.

    • setColor

      public ItemBuilder setColor(String hexColor)
      Sets the color of the ItemStack using a hex color code.
      Parameters:
      hexColor - the hex color string (e.g., "#FF5733").
      Returns:
      the updated ItemBuilder instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the hex color format is invalid.

    • getCustomModelData

      public int getCustomModelData()
      Retrieves the custom model data of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      the custom model data, or -1 if invalid or not set.

    • hasCustomModelData

      public boolean hasCustomModelData()
      Checks if the ItemStack has custom model data.
      Returns:
      true if custom model data is present, otherwise false.

    • setCustomModelData

      public ItemBuilder setCustomModelData(int customModelData)
      Sets the custom model data for the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      customModelData - the custom model data value to set.
      Returns:
      the updated ItemBuilder instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack is invalid.

    • getDamaged

      public int getDamaged()
      Gets the current damage value of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      the amount of damage, or -1 if the ItemStack is invalid or not damageable.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.

    • getMaxDurability

      public int getMaxDurability()
      Gets the maximum durability of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      the maximum durability value, or -1 if the ItemStack is invalid or not damageable.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.

    • hasDamaged

      public boolean hasDamaged()
      Checks if the ItemStack has taken any damage.
      Returns:
      true if the ItemStack has been damaged, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.

    • hasMaxDurability

      public boolean hasMaxDurability()
      Checks if the ItemStack has a maximum durability value set.
      Returns:
      true if the ItemStack has a maximum durability value, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.

    • setDamaged

      public ItemBuilder setDamaged(int amount)
      Sets the damage value of the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      amount - the damage amount to set. Clamped between 0 and the max durability of the ItemStack.
      Returns:
      the modified ItemBuilder instance, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.

    • setMaxDurability

      public ItemBuilder setMaxDurability(int amount)
      Sets the maximum durability of the ItemStack.
      Parameters:
      amount - the maximum durability value to set.
      Returns:
      the modified ItemBuilder instance, or null if the ItemStack is invalid.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the ItemStack does not have Damageable metadata.
